title: Inspect /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public for how Kernel and /vote are currently positioned. Focus on CODEX.md, README.md, .codex/prompts/kernel.md, .codex/prompts/vote.md, scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh, scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh, scripts/local/launchers/*. Return the min...
summary_text: Recommended minimal-safe cleanup: 1. Keep the archive branch, delete feature branch + feature worktrees. - `PR #639` is already merged, so deleting the merged feature branch reduces operator confusion. - The archive branch is a recovery snapshot (`9499be6c7`); keep it for a short retention window (or until rollback confidence is confirmed), then delete it later. 2. Delete remote merged feature branch. - Rationale: avoids duplicate “source of truth” for the same work. - Command: `git push origin --delete codex/kernel-orchestration-hardening-20260408` 3. Delete local feature branch only if merged cleanly. - Rationale: avoid accidental local edits against a branch name that no longer maps to active work. - Command: `git branch -d codex/kernel-orchestration-hardening-20260408` 4. Remove temporary worktrees used for archive/staging. - Rationale: prevents stale paths from being mistakenly used later. - Command: `git worktree remove <archival-worktree-path>` (repeat for all non-primary worktrees tied to completed merge/archive prep) 5. Preserve a rollback point explicitly. - Push/archive branch should already exist and be reachable; optionally add a lightweight tag to it if you want even stronger recovery control. - Rationale: gives one-click recovery without keeping an active feature branch. 6. Final sanity check (one pass). - `git worktree list`, `git branch -a`, `git status --short` - Rationale: confirms only intended worktrees/branches remain and working tree is clean.
